features.name: Simulation engine for per-asset sensor data
features.description: No physical IoT exists. Build a simulation step (module under backend/app/services/, e.g. asset_simulator.py) that generates realistic, evolving values for each asset type's ASSET_READINGS columns on every /workflow/run call -- values should trend (not be purely random each run) so risk stories are visible over multiple runs, per docs/FarmTwin-AI-Copilot.md's 'simulated data should behave realistically over time.'
features.user_visible_behavior: Each POST /workflow/run call (once wired in feat-012) writes one new ASSET_READINGS row per asset, and values drift plausibly run over run rather than jumping randomly.
verification: cd backend && python -m compileall app
Calling the simulator function directly across 5+ simulated ticks for one asset shows a plausible trend (e.g. dissolved oxygen trending down, not random noise) matching a hand-picked scenario
evidence: 2026-07-14: Added backend/app/services/asset_simulator.py: next_reading(asset_type, previous) -- a persisted-last-value + bounded random walk per numeric metric (metric-specific min/max/step/drift), plus dedicated logic for growth_stage (ordered, one-step-at-a-time advance with 15% chance per tick), irrigation_status (derived from soil_moisture_pct), and egg_count (small random walk around the previous count). dissolved_oxygen_mg_l gets one extra directional-drift rule (continues declining below 4.0, gently recovers above 6.0, neutral in between) so the seeded fish-pond crisis continues realistically rather than randomly bouncing back to healthy.
2026-07-14: cd backend && python -m compileall app -- clean.

2026-07-14: Ran a 4-scenario verification script (seeded RNG for reproducibility): (A) fish_pond starting from the real seeded critical DO value (3.5 mg/L) across 8 ticks -- DO declined continuously (3.5 -> 3.24 -> 2.88 -> 2.49 -> 2.38 -> 2.15 -> 2.0, then held at the 2.0 floor), every tick-to-tick delta <= 0.39 (bounded, not random jumps), confirming a plausible worsening trend matching the demo's fish-pond crisis narrative; (B) fish_pond starting from a healthy DO value (7.0) stayed healthy/rose slightly over 5 ticks, confirming stable assets don't spuriously degrade; (C) rice_field growth_stage advanced exactly one stage at a time or held over 20 ticks (seedling -> vegetative -> reproductive -> ripening -> harvest_ready), never skipped or reversed; (D) chicken_coop egg_count/feed_level/water_l all evolved smoothly tick to tick with no implausible jumps.
features.notes: Kept the trend logic simple per the notes below: a persisted last-value + bounded random walk for every metric, plus one scripted directional rule (dissolved_oxygen_mg_l) tied to the specific demo scenario already present in the seed data -- not a general stress-detection framework across all metrics.

features.name: Rewrite /workflow/run as Observe -> Understand -> Recommend -> Predict
features.description: Replace run_daily_workflow's rice-farm logic with the 4-stage cycle per asset: Observe (run the feat-011 simulator + ingest farm-wide weather), Understand (write ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS per asset via the feat-009 agent), Recommend (write RECOMMENDATIONS rows in the full 6-field structured format), Predict (a short-horizon prediction, stored either as an ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS row with a future-looking risk_type or a dedicated notes field -- decide during implementation and record the decision in docs/architecture.md).
features.user_visible_behavior: POST /workflow/run writes real ASSET_READINGS, ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS, and RECOMMENDATIONS rows for all 4 assets and returns a DailyBriefing v2 reflecting real counts, not hardcoded values.
verification: uvicorn app.main:app --reload, then curl -X POST localhost:8000/workflow/run
New rows appear in ASSET_READINGS, ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS, and RECOMMENDATIONS for all 4 assets with today's timestamp
At least one RECOMMENDATIONS row has all 6 fields populated with non-placeholder content
evidence: 2026-07-14: Added backend/app/services/risk_engine.py (assess_risk: rule-based Understand stage using the same thresholds the agent itself was instructed to use -- DO<3.5=critical/<5.0=high/<6.0=medium, water temp>32C, disease>40%/20%, etc. -- across all 4 asset types; predict_trend: Predict stage, compares the new reading to the previous one and returns a one-sentence linear projection only when the driving metric is moving in the worsening direction) and backend/app/services/recommendation_parser.py (parse_recommendations: line-based regex extraction of the agent's 6 bolded field labels into structured dicts, tolerant of surrounding formatting variance).

2026-07-14: Rewrote backend/app/main.py's /workflow/run as Observe (simulate + write ASSET_READINGS via feat-011's simulator; fetch + write farm-wide WEATHER_READINGS, extended weather_client.get_today_reading to also pull wind_speed_10m_max) -> Understand (risk_engine.assess_risk, write ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS) -> Recommend (for medium+ risk assets only: real FARM_OPS_AGENT call with an asset-specific prompt, parsed into RECOMMENDATIONS rows) -> Predict (risk_engine.predict_trend, written as an ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS row with risk_type suffixed _forecast_24h). Added settings.farm_lat/farm_lon to config.py since FARM_ASSETS has no lat/lon post-pivot (one physical farm, not many geo-distributed plots) -- weather now needs a single configured location.

2026-07-14: Verified parse_recommendations() against the real 4-recommendation FP-001 response captured in feat-009's evidence (reconstructed as a fixture) -- correctly extracted all 4 blocks with all 6 fields each, matching priorities/confidences exactly.

2026-07-14: Ran /workflow/run against the live account 3 times while iterating (uvicorn started/stopped cleanly each time, port 8000 checked first). Run 1 (HTTP 200, 89.6s): assets_assessed=4, high_risk_assets=['FP-001'] (only the seeded crisis asset flagged, as intended), 3 RECOMMENDATIONS rows written with real distinct priority/confidence/reasoning text -- but the summary field leaked the agent's raw tool-call narration ('I'll assess... Let me query...') ahead of an inconsistent <answer> tag. Run 2 hit a genuine httpx.ReadTimeout at the 90s client timeout (same known Cortex Agent flakiness documented in feat-006/feat-009) -- bumped cortex_agent_client.py's timeout 90s->150s, same fix pattern as the prior build. Fixed the narration leak by discovering the wrapper format isn't consistent across calls (sometimes an explicit <answer> tag, sometimes narration runs straight into the first markdown heading with no tag) -- rewrote _clean_agent_answer() to handle both shapes plus a no-heading fallback, verified against 3 fixture shapes (tagged, untagged-with-heading, plain) all correct. Run 3 (HTTP 200, 100.4s) and run 4 (HTTP 200, 100.4s) both produced clean summaries starting directly at the structured report, zero narration leakage.

2026-07-14: Cross-checked Snowflake directly after run 4: ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS for FP-001 showed correctly alternating current (dissolved_oxygen, critical) and forecast (dissolved_oxygen_forecast_24h, critical, real linear-projection text) rows each tick; RECOMMENDATIONS accumulated 9 total rows across the 3 successful runs (3 per run, all for FP-001, all pending_approval); table counts grew as expected (ASSET_READINGS 120->135, ASSET_RISK_ASSESSMENTS 22->40, WEATHER_READINGS 0->4).
features.notes: This is the direct replacement for the old feat-002/003/004 pipeline, now covering 4 heterogeneous assets in one pass instead of N identical rice farms. Known scope limitation (not fixed, matching this repo's precedent of documenting rather than over-engineering around hackathon-scale limits): the per-asset loop has no cross-asset transaction/rollback -- if the agent call for one at-risk asset fails, assets later in ASSET_ID order that haven't been processed yet simply get caught up on the next /workflow/run call rather than the whole run atomically failing. Each Cortex Agent call is real and can take 60-150s; with only one asset at risk at a time (per the demo's 'one dramatic risk event' scope) this stays within a single request, but N simultaneous at-risk assets would serialize to N times that -- acceptable for this hackathon's one-asset-crisis demo, not for a general multi-incident production workflow.

features.name: Reuse Snowflake connections instead of reconnecting per query
features.description: backend/app/services/snowflake_client.py's run_query()/execute()/execute_many() each call get_connection() fresh and close() it immediately after every single call. A single GET /dashboard/summary issues 4 sequential queries (assets, alerts, tasks, weather) and GET /assets/{id} issues 5 -- each paying a full Snowflake connection handshake (auth + session init + warehouse resume) on every one. Replace with a reused connection (a lazily-created module-level connection, reconnecting only on error/staleness -- or a small connection pool if a bare module-level connection proves unsafe under concurrent requests) so one HTTP request pays that handshake cost once, not once per query.
features.user_visible_behavior: GET /dashboard/summary and other multi-query endpoints respond noticeably faster.
verification: Time GET /dashboard/summary before and after the change against the live account with curl -w '%{time_total}'; record both numbers

Cross-check a few /dashboard/summary and /assets/{id} field values against direct Snowflake SELECTs after the change to confirm no correctness regression from connection reuse (e.g. stale reads, uncommitted transaction leakage across requests)
evidence: 2026-07-14: Rewrote backend/app/services/snowflake_client.py: get_connection() now returns a thread-local (threading.local()) connection, created lazily and reused across queries/requests on that thread instead of a fresh snowflake.connector.connect() + close() on every single call; recreates only if the stored connection reports is_closed(). run_query/execute/execute_many no longer close the connection after use.
2026-07-14: cd backend && python -m compileall app -- clean.

2026-07-14: Measured GET /dashboard/summary (4 sequential queries per request) against the live account, 5 calls each, by temporarily git-stash-ing the fix to get a true baseline on the original code, then popping the stash back: BEFORE (fresh connection per query): 4.36s, 3.76s, 3.24s, 3.85s, 2.89s -- consistently 3-4.4s per call. AFTER (thread-local reuse): 1.46s (first call on a cold thread, paying one handshake instead of four), then 0.34s, 0.46s, 0.33s, 0.34s once the thread's connection was warm -- roughly an 85%+ reduction after warm-up, and ~3x faster even on the very first cold call.

2026-07-14: Correctness verified live: GET /assets cross-checked field-for-field against a direct run_query() SELECT on FARM_ASSETS -- exact match. A live approve (POST /recommendations/{id}/approve) followed immediately by a GET /assets/{id}/recommendations on the same reused connection correctly showed the pending count drop from 2 to 1 -- confirms writes commit and are immediately visible on the reused connection, no transaction-leakage or stale-read issue. Fired 8 concurrent GET /assets requests (background curls) and confirmed all 8 returned identical, correct 4-asset data with no corruption or cross-thread interference from the thread-local design.
2026-07-14: Stopped uvicorn cleanly after verification (both the baseline-measurement run and the final fixed-code run).
features.notes: Root-caused during the 2026-07-14 performance investigation: the user reported the dashboard as slow and asked for 'cache or React hooks', but the dominant cost was backend-side connection overhead, not query logic or a lack of frontend caching. This was the primary fix for first-load latency; feat-021's frontend cache remains a complementary fix for repeat-navigation latency, not a substitute for this. Accepted, documented scope limit: a connection idle long enough to expire server-side (well beyond a demo session's timespan) would surface as a query error on next use rather than being silently retried -- no retry/backoff logic was added, matching this repo's precedent of not over-engineering past hackathon-scale needs.

features.name: Shared data-fetching cache/hook to reduce redundant round-trips
features.description: Dashboard, the CopilotPanel's priorities feed, and the new split Farm view (feat-022) each call GET /dashboard/summary independently today, triggering a fresh Snowflake round-trip every time even when the data was fetched seconds ago elsewhere in the app. Build a small dependency-free shared fetch cache (e.g. frontend/lib/useApiData.ts: a React hook backed by a module-level cache keyed by URL, with in-flight request de-duplication, a short staleness TTL, and a manual invalidate() called after any mutation -- approve/reject, /workflow/run) so navigating between screens or reopening the copilot panel doesn't always pay a fresh live-data cost, while staying correct immediately after a real action changes the data.
features.user_visible_behavior: Switching between the dashboard/asset panel and reopening the Copilot panel feels instant on repeat visits within the cache window; approving/rejecting a recommendation still shows the fresh state everywhere (dashboard, copilot panel, asset panel) immediately, not stale cached data.
verification: npm run build && npm run lint succeed in frontend/

Playwright: load the dashboard, note network calls; navigate away and back within the TTL window, confirm no duplicate GET /dashboard/summary fired; approve a recommendation, confirm the dashboard/copilot/asset views all reflect the change without a manual page refresh
evidence: 2026-07-14: Added frontend/lib/dataCache.ts (module-level store keyed by string: cached value + timestamp, in-flight-request de-duplication, a 20s default staleness TTL, and invalidate(key) which clears the entry and immediately re-fetches it in the background using the last-registered fetcher for that key, so every subscribed component picks up fresh data without needing to know a mutation happened elsewhere) and frontend/lib/useApiData.ts (a hook wrapping it via React 18+'s built-in useSyncExternalStore -- no new dependency, per the user's explicit 'stay dependency-free' direction).

2026-07-14: Wired frontend/app/dashboard/page.tsx and frontend/components/CopilotPanel.tsx to both call useApiData('dashboard-summary', getDashboardSummary) -- same cache key, so the two independently-mounted consumers share one fetch instead of two. Wired frontend/app/assets/[id]/page.tsx's handleDecision (feat-017) to call invalidate('dashboard-summary') after a successful approve/reject, so the Dashboard page and Copilot panel refresh automatically on next mount/subscription notification.

2026-07-14: Hit two React 19 lint errors on the first pass (this project's frontend/AGENTS.md already warns this Next.js/React version has stricter hook rules than training data): 'Cannot access refs during render' (writing fetcherRef.current = fetcher directly in the render body) and 'Calling setState synchronously within an effect' (setError(null) called synchronously at the top of the load effect, before the async call). Fixed by moving the ref write into its own no-dependency-array useEffect (runs after render, not during) and restructuring the load effect so all setState calls happen inside the load().then()/.catch()/.finally() chain instead of synchronously at the top. npm run build and npm run lint both clean after.
